title: PRD-015: Config Validation and Remote Loading

overview.description: Add configuration validation and remote config loading to help teams share and validate jarvy.toml configurations.
goals: Validate jarvy.toml before running setup to catch errors early
Provide helpful error messages with suggestions for typos
Load configs from HTTP(S) URLs for team sharing
Support authenticated requests for private configs

userStories.title: Config Validation
userStories.description: As a config author, I want to validate my jarvy.toml before sharing so that team members don't encounter errors.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: TOML syntax errors show line numbers
Hooks referencing unknown tools produce warnings
Exit code 0 for valid, non-zero for errors
Validation completes in <100ms for local files

userStories.title: Remote Config Loading
userStories.description: As a team lead, I want to host our team's jarvy.toml on a server so that all team members use the same environment configuration.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: jarvy setup --from <URL> fetches and uses remote config
HTTPS URLs work with valid certificates
HTTP URLs rejected in release builds
--header flag allows custom headers for auth
Clear error messages for network failures
Size limit enforced (1MB) to prevent memory exhaustion

functionalRequirements.description: jarvy validate command checks local jarvy.toml
Unknown tool names detected with fuzzy matching suggestions
TOML syntax errors include line numbers
jarvy setup --from URL fetches remote configs
HTTPS enforced for remote configs (HTTP only in debug)
Custom headers supported for authenticated requests

technicalConsiderations.details: Use strsim crate or simple Levenshtein distance for typo suggestions
Use reqwest with minimal features (rustls-tls) to keep binary size small
Expose list of known tools from registry for validation
No arbitrary code execution from configs; only declarative tool specs

implementationSteps.description: Create src/validate/mod.rs with ValidationResult, ValidationError structs
Implement known-tools registry check (expose tool names from registry)
Add fuzzy matching for tool name suggestions using strsim
Implement validate_config() function with all checks
Add jarvy validate CLI command with --config and --strict flags
Add reqwest dependency with minimal features
Implement remote config fetching with HTTPS validation
Add --from and --header flags to jarvy setup command
Add --from flag to jarvy validate command
Write tests for remote config fetching

risks.mitigation: Clear error messages; users can fall back to local config
HTTPS only, validation before use, no code execution
